generated_oml:
import DTDFVocab

instance LiDARDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized LiDAR point‑cloud data following ISO 23247 translation, converted into ROS 2 messages and published over the local network."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized vehicle state stream (speed, acceleration, steering angle) with unit conversions per ISO 23247."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuatorDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized actuation command stream for motor and servo control, aligned to ROS 2 topic conventions."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuatorSensor
]

instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High‑resolution LiDAR sensor delivering raw 3D point‑clouds from the vehicle’s physical environment, used for point‑cloud clustering & tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARDataTransmission
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle state sensor capturing speed, acceleration, and steering angle for real‑time virtual representation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmission
]

instance ActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator monitoring sensor capturing motor and servo actuation data for feedback into the twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuatorDataTransmission
]

instance LiDARTrackingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR tracking data: point‑cloud clustering & tracking outputs discrete object tracks projected onto a 2D plane."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARDataProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateProcessedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state after ISO translation, unit conversion, and type alignment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateDataProcessingEnabler
]

instance ActuatorCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuation data after serialization, ready for downstream control logic."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuatorDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuatorDataProcessingEnabler
]

instance LiDARDataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Performs point‑cloud clustering & tracking on LiDARTrackingData, preparing data for visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance VehicleStateDataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processes vehicle state data for monitoring and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ActuatorDataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Prepares actuation commands for downstream execution."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance AccComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance RemoteCommandEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real‑time monitoring of vehicle state, projected trajectory and detected objects via the UI."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, TrackedObjectsInsight, ProjectedTrajectoryInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [

    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

    base:desc "Insight into real‑time vehicle metrics (speed, acceleration, steering angle) displayed in the cockpit."
]

instance TrackedObjectsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into tracked objects detected by LiDAR point‑cloud clustering for situational awareness."
]

    base:desc "Insight into projected vehicle trajectory, showing planned driving path."
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    base:desc "User interface front‑end providing real‑time monitoring and remote control capabilities."
]

instance PointCloudClusteringFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Subset of the Digital Twin Figure of Components responsible for point‑cloud clustering & tracking."
]

instance VisualizationPreprocessingFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Visualization pre‑processing layer that transforms processed Data into 3‑D cockpit rendering."
]

instance DataStorageFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component managing SQL database storage of historical vehicle state for reporting and analytics."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a rigorous clock synchronization scheme, requiring all participating device compute nodes to align their operating time within a precision of 100 ms—this constraint is dictated by hardware limitations and aligned with safety‑critical vehicular standards that demand sub‑100‑ms synchronization. Synchronization is enforced across the ROS2‑based device network, ensuring that data exchange between physical and virtual components adheres to this temporal granularity."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows the ISO 23247 “Digital Twin framework for manufacturing” by arranging the system into entity‑based Functional Entities (FEs). Key software components are first identified, then mapped onto FEs and implemented as ROS 2 packages containing ROS 2 nodes. The User Entity groups user‑facing FEs—specifically an interface FE split into visualization and control panel. The Digital Twin Entity aggregates data‑management and control FEs, while the Physical Twin Entity, distinct from the ISO 23247 Physical Twin concept, hosts resource‑specific and general automotive FEs such as point‑cloud clustering and tracking. Deployment occurs across separate hardware, ensuring decentralized execution. Coordination mechanisms include 100 ms clock synchronization, network latency limits, and inter‑FE messaging via ROS 2 topics/services, thereby providing coherent real‑time interaction across all entities.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Software components communicate over a ROS2 networking stack, exchanging data via DDS topics. Inter‑entity communication between the physical twin and the digital twin occurs over Wi‑Fi using the UDP transport protocol. The document does not specify bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy or security mechanisms beyond the use of ROS2 QoS policies and Wi‑Fi UDP."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "ISO 23247 Part 2 defines an entity‑based reference architecture with four core entities—Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity.  Inter‑entity interfaces are specified as collections of Functional Entities (FEs) that can be implemented with any technology stack; for example, the standard cites OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) as a possible communication layer."
]
